questions.question: What is a formula in Excel?
options: An expression that begins with an equal sign and performs calculations
questions.explanation: The module: 'Formulas are entered in the worksheet cell and must begin with an equal sign “=”' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What does the SUM function do in Excel?
options: Finds the sum of a range of cells
Finds the average of a range
questions.explanation: The module: 'SUM function and simply type “=SUM(D1:D10)”' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What does the AVERAGE function do in Excel?
options: Finds the average of a range of cells
Finds the sum of a range
questions.explanation: The module: 'AVERAGE: finds the average of cells B1 through B10' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What does the MAX function do in Excel?
options: Returns the highest number from a range
questions.explanation: The module: 'MAX: returns the highest number from cells C1 through C100' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What does the MIN function do in Excel?
options: Returns the lowest number from a range
questions.explanation: The module: 'MIN: returns the lowest number from cells D1 through D100' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What does the SQRT function do in Excel?
options: Finds the square root of a value
Finds the square of a value
Finds the power of a value
questions.explanation: The module: 'SQRT: finds the square root of the value in cell D10' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What does the TODAY function do in Excel?
questions.explanation: The module: 'TODAY: returns the current date' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you sort data in Excel?
options: Highlight cells and click Sort Ascending or Sort Descending on the toolbar
questions.explanation: The module describes both basic and complex sorts. For basic: 'highlight the cells that will be sorted and click the Sort Ascending (A-Z) button or Sort Descending (Z-A) button on the standard toolbar.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is the Autofill feature in Excel?
options: Allows you to quickly fill cells with repetitive or sequential data
questions.explanation: The module: 'The Autofill feature allows you to quickly fill cells with repetitive or sequential data such as chronological dates or numbers, and repeated text.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you insert a clip art image in Excel?
options: Insert > Picture > Clip Art from the menu bar
questions.explanation: The module: 'Select Insert|Picture|Clip Art from the menu bar.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you create a chart in Excel using the Chart Wizard?
options: Select data, click Chart Wizard, follow steps
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click the Chart Wizard button on the standard toolbar to view the first Chart Wizard dialogue box.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you set page breaks in Excel?
options: Select the row, then Insert > Page Break
questions.explanation: The module: 'To set page breaks within the worksheet, select the row you want to appear just below the page break by clicking the row’s label. Then choose Insert|Page Break from the menu bar.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you change page orientation in Excel?
options: File > Page Setup > Page tab > Orientation
questions.explanation: The module: 'Select the Orientation under the Page tab in the Page Setup window to make the page Landscape or Portrait.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you print a worksheet in Excel?
questions.explanation: The module: 'To print the worksheet, select File|Print from the menu bar.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is a macro in Excel?
options: A recorded sequence of actions that can be replayed
questions.explanation: The module: 'Macros can speed up any common editing sequence you may execute in an Excel spreadsheet.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is a software suite?
options: A collection of individual programs available together as a unit
questions.explanation: The module: 'A software suite is a collection of individual programs available together as a unit.' (Unit 5, Session 1, but also in Unit 2; we'll cite Unit 5 Session 1)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Paste Options button in Word?
options: Provides different options depending on the source of the content
questions.explanation: The module: 'When you paste content, the Paste Options button provides different options, depending on the source of the content.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What does the 'Keep Source Formatting' paste option do?
options: Preserves the look of the original text
questions.explanation: The module: 'Keep Source Formatting: This option preserves the look of the original text.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What does the 'Keep Text Only' paste option do?
options: Removes all the original formatting from the text
Preserves the look of the original text
questions.explanation: The module: 'Keep Text Only: This option removes all the original formatting from the text.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: How do you insert a chart in a Word document?
options: There is no mention; but we can ask about Excel. We'll skip.
Instead, we'll ask: How do you insert a SmartArt graphic? (already done) We'll add more questions on Spreadsheet.
questions.explanation: The module does not cover chart insertion in Word. We'll focus on Excel chart creation.

questions.question: What is a worksheet in Excel?
options: A data file created with spreadsheet software
questions.explanation: The module: 'A Worksheet is the data file created with spreadsheet software.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you insert a row in Excel?
options: Select Insert > Rows from the menu bar, or right-click and choose Insert
questions.explanation: The module: 'To add a row to a worksheet, select Insert|Rows from the menu bar, or highlight the row by clicking on the row label, right-click with the mouse, and choose Insert.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you copy cell contents in Excel?
options: Select Edit > Copy or click the Copy button
questions.explanation: The module: 'To copy the cell contents, select Edit|Copy from the menu bar or click the Copy button on the standard toolbar.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Format Painter in Excel?
options: Copies formatting from one cell to another
questions.explanation: The module: 'Format Painter ... If you have formatted a cell with a certain font style, date format, border, and other formatting options, and you want to format another cell ... click the Format Painter button.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is a chart in Excel?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Charts allow you to present data entered into the worksheet in a visual format using a variety of graph types.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you move a chart in Excel?
options: Select the border of the chart and drag to a new location
questions.explanation: The module: 'Select the border of the chart, hold down the left mouse button, and drag the chart to a new location.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Legend in a chart?
options: Explains what each colour or pattern represents
questions.explanation: The module explains that a legend is used to state what each series represents. (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you add a header or footer in Excel?
questions.explanation: The module: 'To modify a preset header or footer, or to make your own, click the Custom Header and Custom Footer buttons.' under Page Setup. (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: How do you print gridlines in Excel?
options: Check Gridlines in the Sheet tab of Page Setup
questions.explanation: The module: 'Check Gridlines if you want the gridlines dividing the cells to be printed on the page.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is presentation software?
options: Software that allows users to create and present visual presentations (e.g., PowerPoint)
questions.explanation: The unit is titled 'Introduction to Presentation Software' and focuses on Microsoft PowerPoint. (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the Ribbon in PowerPoint?
options: A series of tabs that organize related functions into groups
questions.explanation: The module: 'The ribbon, which spans across the top of the PowerPoint screen, replaces the old Menu Bar and toolbars... It organizes related functions into tabs.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the Quick Access Toolbar in PowerPoint?
options: A customizable toolbar that provides access to commonly used functions like Save or Undo
questions.explanation: The module: 'This is a customizable toolbar that provides access to commonly used functions such as Save or Undo Last Action.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What are Contextual Command Tabs in PowerPoint?
options: Tools that appear directly above a tab and provide access to options pertaining to a just-completed action
questions.explanation: The module: 'Tools appear directly above a tab and provide access to options that pertain to a just-completed action.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the Slide Pane in PowerPoint?
options: The large white typing area where you type slide text
The area for viewing slide show
questions.explanation: The module: 'The large white typing area that features “Click to add…” text prompts. This is the area where you’ll type your slide text.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the Slides Tab in PowerPoint?
options: Displays thumbnail versions of slides in your presentation
questions.explanation: The module: 'Located to the left of the slide pane. Displays thumbnail versions of slides in your presentation.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the Notes Pane in PowerPoint?
options: Lets you type speaker notes for each slide
Is where you type slide text
Is where you view the slide show
questions.explanation: The module: 'Located beneath the slide pane. Lets you type speaker notes for each slide.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What are Text Placeholders in PowerPoint?
options: Prompts like 'Click to add...' where you enter text
questions.explanation: The module: 'Located inside the slide. (Look for “Click to add...” prompts)' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the Title Bar in PowerPoint?
options: Displays the program title and filename
questions.explanation: The module: 'Located at the very top of the screen. Displays the program title as well as the filename you assign your presentation upon saving it.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the Task Bar (Status Bar) in PowerPoint?
options: Displays slide number and allows zoom and view changes
questions.explanation: The module: 'Located at the bottom of the screen. The Taskbar displays the number of the current slide... It also allows you to zoom in and out... and to change your slide view.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What are the vertical and horizontal scroll bars used for in PowerPoint?
options: To scroll through slides and zoomed-in areas
questions.explanation: The module: 'The vertical scroll bar allows you to scroll through your slides. The horizontal scroll bar appears when you zoom in on part of the slide.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What are the page up/page down buttons in PowerPoint?
options: Move you slide-by-slide in either direction
Move to the first and last slide
questions.explanation: The module: 'Located beneath the vertical scroll bar... Moves you slide-by-slide in either direction.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you create a title slide in PowerPoint?
options: Follow the on-screen prompts 'Click to add...'
questions.explanation: The module: 'Follow PowerPoint’s on-screen prompts to enter text (“Click to add...” ).' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you save a PowerPoint presentation?
options: Choose Save from the Quick Access toolbar
questions.explanation: The module: 'Choose Save from the Quick Access toolbar.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you create a new slide in PowerPoint?
options: Click on New Slide from the Home tab
questions.explanation: The module: '1. Click on New Slide from the Home tab.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the default slide layout for a new slide in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module shows the 'Title and Content' layout as the most common, and it's the default option when you click New Slide. (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you add text to a shape in PowerPoint?
options: Right-click inside the shape and select Edit Text
questions.explanation: The module: '1. Right-mouse click inside the shape. 2. Select Edit Text.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you insert a text box in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: '1. Click on the Insert tab and select Text Box from the Text group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you insert clip art in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Insert tab and choose Clip Art from the Illustrations group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you insert a picture from a file in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Insert tab and choose Picture from the Illustrations group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you crop an image in PowerPoint?
options: Select the image, then Picture Tools > Format > Crop
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Crop icon on the Picture Tools tab.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you create a hyperlink in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Insert tab and choose Hyperlink from the Links group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you rearrange slides using the Slide Sorter in PowerPoint?
options: View tab > Slide Sorter, then drag slides to new positions
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the View tab and choose Slide Sorter from the Presentation Views group. ... Position the mouse pointer over the slide you wish to relocate, and then click and hold down the mouse... drag the mouse... let go.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you create slide transitions in PowerPoint?
options: Animations tab > Transition to This Slide group
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Animations tab.' and then 'Glide your mouse over each of the transition effects located in the Transition to This Slide group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you apply a transition to all slides in PowerPoint?
options: After selecting a transition, click Apply To All
questions.explanation: The module: 'To apply the transition to the entire presentation, click on Apply To All.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you view a slide show in PowerPoint?
options: Slide Show tab > From Beginning or From Current Slide
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Slide Show tab. Click on From Beginning to view the entire presentation or From Current Slide.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you print a PowerPoint presentation?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Microsoft Office button and choose Print.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you print audience handouts in PowerPoint?
options: In Print, choose Handouts and select slides per page
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the down arrow by the Print What window. Choose Handouts. Click on the down arrow by the Slides per page window. Select the number of slides you want to appear on each page.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you create speaker notes in PowerPoint?
options: Click in the 'Click to add notes' pane beneath the slide
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click in the Click to add notes pane directly beneath your slides to add notes.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you print speaker notes in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the down arrow by the window labelled Print What. Choose Notes Pages. Click OK.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you spell-check a PowerPoint presentation?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Review tab and choose Spelling from the Proofing group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you get online help in PowerPoint?
options: Click the Help icon (question mark) and type a topic
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click the Help icon, located directly beneath the x in the upper right corner of the screen. Type a topic... Choose Search.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you exit PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Microsoft Office button and choose Exit PowerPoint.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you close a presentation without exiting PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Microsoft Office button and choose Close.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you open a saved presentation in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Microsoft Office button and choose Open.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Slide Sorter view in PowerPoint?
options: To view and rearrange slides in miniature form
questions.explanation: The module: 'The Slide Sorter displays the slides of your presentation side-by-side in miniature.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you add a shape in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Insert tab and choose Shapes from the Illustrations group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you resize a shape in PowerPoint?
options: Use the Size options on the Format tab
questions.explanation: The module explains resizing: 'Position the mouse pointer tip directly over any of the corner handles... drag the mouse in a diagonal direction.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you change the colour of a shape in PowerPoint?
options: Select the shape, then Home > Shape Fill
questions.explanation: The module: 'Change the colour and/or outline of the shape by selecting Shape Fill.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Crop tool in PowerPoint?
options: To remove unwanted edges of a picture
questions.explanation: The module: 'Cropping reduces the size of a picture by removing vertical or horizontal edges.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you insert a hyperlink to a website in PowerPoint?
options: Insert tab > Hyperlink, then enter the web address
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Insert tab and choose Hyperlink from the Links group. ... Enter the web address in the Address field.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you animate a bulleted list in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Animations tab. Choose Custom Animation from the Animations group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you add an entrance effect to an animation in PowerPoint?
options: In Custom Animation, click Add Effects > Entrance
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Add Effects tab. Select Entrance.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you dim previous bulleted points in PowerPoint?
options: In Custom Animation, select Effect Options > After animation > choose colour
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the down arrow by the animation listing. Choose Effect Options… Click on the down arrow by the After animation window. Click on a colour choice for dimming the previous bullets.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you create a chart in PowerPoint?
options: Insert tab > Chart, then select chart type
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Chart icon in the centre of the slide... Select a chart type... click OK.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you edit a chart's data in PowerPoint?
options: Click the chart, then Chart Tools > Design > Edit Data
questions.explanation: The module: 'Make sure the Design tab under Chart Tools is selected and then click on Edit Data from the Data group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you change the chart type in PowerPoint?
options: Select chart, Chart Tools > Design > Change Chart Type
questions.explanation: The module: 'Make sure the Design tab under Chart Tools is selected and then click on Change Chart Type from Type Group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you delete the legend in a PowerPoint chart?
options: Select chart, Chart Tools > Layout > Legend > None
questions.explanation: The module: 'Make sure the Layout tab under Chart Tools is selected and then click on Legend from the Labels group. ... select None to delete it.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you resize a chart in PowerPoint?
options: Drag the corner handles of the selection box
Use the Size options on the Format tab
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on any of the four corners of the selection box... drag the mouse button diagonally to stretch or shrink the chart area.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you apply a contrasting colour to the chart's background in PowerPoint?
options: Right-click chart > Fill > Solid fill, then choose colour
questions.explanation: The module: 'Right-click on the chart ... Select Fill ... choose Solid fill ... Click on the down arrow by the paint bucket ... choose a colour.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you change the colour of a line in a line chart in PowerPoint?
options: Select the line, then Chart Tools > Format > Shape Outline
questions.explanation: The module: 'Choose Format beneath the Chart Tools tab and click on Shape Outline. Choose a colour for your chart line.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you insert a chart in PowerPoint? (Same as earlier but ask about menu)
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Insert tab and choose Chart from the Illustrations group?' Actually it's not explicitly stated but the process is to click the Chart icon. We'll ask: 'How do you insert a chart in PowerPoint?' with options: Insert > Chart, Home > Chart, etc. But we already have a similar question. Let's ask about the Chart icon on the slide. We'll vary.

questions.question: What appears when you click the Chart icon on a slide in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Select a chart type from the list on the left side of the Insert Chart dialogue box.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the difference between Slide Sorter and Normal view?
options: Slide Sorter shows thumbnails; Normal view shows a single slide with editing panes
questions.explanation: The module describes Normal view (Slide pane, Notes pane, Thumbnails) and Slide Sorter (thumbnails only). (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you add a transition to a slide in PowerPoint?
options: Animations tab > select a transition effect
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Animations tab. Glide your mouse over each of the transition effects located in the Transition to This Slide group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the purpose of speaker notes in PowerPoint?
options: To provide additional information for the presenter to speak
questions.explanation: The module: 'Lets you type speaker notes for each slide. When printed out, the slide image and notes for that slide appear together on the same page.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you advance from slide to slide during a slide show?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the left mouse button to advance from slide to slide.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you exit a slide show before the end?
options: Click the right mouse button and choose End Show
questions.explanation: The module: 'You can press Escape on the keyboard at any time to exit the presentation.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you apply a transition to all slides in PowerPoint?
options: Select a transition, then click Apply To All
Select all slides and apply transition
questions.explanation: The module: 'To apply the transition to the entire presentation, click on Apply To All.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Quick Access Toolbar?
options: To provide access to commonly used commands like Save and Undo
questions.explanation: The module: 'This is a customizable toolbar that provides access to commonly used functions such as Save or Undo Last Action.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you add a text box to a slide in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Insert tab and select Text Box from the Text group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you insert a picture from the web into PowerPoint?
options: Insert tab > Picture, then navigate to the saved image
Copy and paste from the web
questions.explanation: The module describes importing an image from the web: 'At the website: position the mouse on the image and click with the right mouse button. Choose Save Image As… In PowerPoint: click Insert > Picture.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you add a shape to a slide in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Click on the Insert tab and choose Shapes from the Illustrations group.' (Unit 6)

questions.question: How do you change the shape fill colour in PowerPoint?
options: Select shape, then Home > Shape Fill
questions.explanation: The module: 'Change the colour and/or outline of the shape by selecting Shape Fill.' (Unit 6)
